name: Camarzana de Tera and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop
name_original: Por el río Tera — Benavente y los Valles
description: This difficult 18.8-mile touring cycling route along the Tera River offers diverse landscapes and passes by the Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Camarzana de Tera traverse a tranquil rural environment, shaped significantly by the Tera River. The landscape features lush valleys, forests of oak and chestnut trees, and open fields. This varied terrain provides a scenic backdrop for outdoor activities, with routes often following the river and offering views of the surrounding countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Camarzana de Tera The most popular touring cycling route is The Benavente river vegas — Benavente, a 39.1 miles (62.9 km) trail that takes 4 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route explores the river vegas, offering a moderate challenge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is The Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias, a moderate 29.1 miles (46.8 km) path. This route circles through the Sierra de Carpurias, featuring varied terrain. Local touring cyclists also love the Camarzana de Tera and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop, a 18.8 miles (30.2 km) trail leading through the Tera Valley and past the Agavanzal Reservoir, often completed in about 2 hours 23 minutes. Touring cycling around Camarzana de Tera is defined by the Tera River, green valleys, and a mix of forests and open fields. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging routes. The routes in Camarzana de Tera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from a few reviews. More than 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Camarzana de Tera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Camarzana de Tera?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;11 touring cycling routes&lt;/strong&gt; around Camarzana de Tera, offering a variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of the touring cycling routes in the area?
A: The routes cater to various abilities, with 4 easy routes, 5 moderate routes, and 2 more challenging options. This ensures there's something for every touring cyclist, from beginners to those seeking a greater challenge.

Q: What natural features can I expect to see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes often follow the picturesque Tera River, providing stunning views and a lush, green environment. You'll also encounter forests of oak and chestnut trees, open fields, and beautiful valleys. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1383988478/camarzana-de-tera-and-agavanzal-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera and Agavanzal Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, pass by significant natural landmarks such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3834108" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural sites to visit while cycling in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa Marta de Tera&lt;/a&gt;, a Romanesque church with the earliest known sculptural representation of St. James as a pilgrim. The area is also part of the Camino Sanabrés, a branch of the Camino de Santiago, adding a cultural layer to your cycling experience. The Roman Villa of Orpheus, an important archaeological site, is also nearby.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Yes, many routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1037613425/the-castle-of-the-counts-of-benavente-from-brime-de-urz-circular-through-the-sierra-de-carpurias" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of the Counts of Benavente from Brime de Urz – circular through the Sierra de Carpurias&lt;/a&gt;, which offers varied terrain. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40010269" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camarzana de Tera – Camarzana de Tera loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Camarzana de Tera?
A: The region's lush, green environment, shaped by the Tera River, makes it pleasant for cycling during spring and autumn when temperatures are mild. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along the river, but it's advisable to cycle during cooler parts of the day.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy routes that are suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature gentler terrain and allow for a leisurely exploration of the tranquil rural environment.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the touring cycling routes?
A: While the region offers many natural paths and open fields ideal for outdoor activities, specific regulations for dogs on cycling routes can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas or near wildlife. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and doesn't disturb other users or local fauna.

Q: Where can I find places to eat or stay near the touring cycling routes?
A: The town of Camarzana de Tera and nearby villages like Mózar offer options for refreshments and accommodation. You might find local cafes, pubs, and guesthouses. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6309776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Anita Tourist Hostel&lt;/a&gt; is a known spot for travelers.

Q: Is there parking available for cyclists in Camarzana de Tera?
A: Yes, as a rural municipality, Camarzana de Tera typically offers public parking options in the town center or near popular starting points for routes. It's advisable to look for designated parking areas or inquire locally upon arrival.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Camarzana de Tera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Camarzana de Tera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the tranquil rural environment, the scenic beauty of the Tera River, and the varied terrain that offers something for everyone.

Q: Are there any routes that are particularly good for beginners?
A: Yes, there are 4 easy routes available, perfect for beginners or those who prefer a less strenuous ride. These routes allow you to enjoy the beautiful landscapes of the Tera Valley without significant elevation changes, such as sections of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6112661" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tera River Trail&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: A small town in the Tera river valley. These fertile agricultural lands have been occupied since Celtic times. The Romans also occupied this area, leaving the remains of a beautiful Roman villa 'Villa Romana de Orpheus' with a mosaic floor.
The reservoir of Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal is located in the municipality of Olleros de Tera. It dams the waters of the Tera River.

Dated between the 2nd and 4th centuries AD. C., consists of a building with fifteen rooms around a central patio. All of them have mosaics inside, mostly with geometric decoration. However, we find two rooms in which mythological characters are represented.
Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest, this archaeological site has been known since the 19th century. XIX, when in 1861 the discovery of coins and other remains during the work on the road from Benavente to Mombuey was published. It was not until 2007 that the excavation began, with the museum opening in 2018.

faqs.question: What is the overall difficulty of the By the Tera River — Benavente and the Valles touring cycling route?
faqs.answer: This specific touring cycling route is rated as difficult. While the broader Benavente y Los Valles region offers trails of varying difficulties, this particular route presents a significant challenge, suitable for experienced cyclists with good fitness.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The route follows the Tera River through the Benavente and Valles region, characterized by undulating hills. You'll encounter a mix of landscapes, from sections with holm oaks at lower elevations to Pyrenean oaks higher up, and open areas. The presence of rivers and valleys is a defining feature, offering diverse scenery.

faqs.question: What are some notable points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll pass by several interesting spots. Highlights include the town of Camarzana de Tera, the scenic Nuestra Señora del Agavanzal Reservoir, and the historical Roman Villa of Orpheus.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region experiences varied weather. While specific recommendations depend on personal preference, spring and autumn generally offer more moderate temperatures and pleasant conditions for cycling. Summer can be hot, and parts of the area were affected by fires in 2022, though recovery is ongoing.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with other established trails. Approximately 21% of it follows the Camino Sanabrés (East), and 18% overlaps with both the Camino Sanabrés and the Mtb Camino Sanabrés (Este) / Mtb Camiño Xabrés (Leste).

faqs.question: Is the By the Tera River — Benavente and the Valles route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the general region is welcoming to outdoor activities, specific dog regulations for this particular touring cycling route are not detailed. It's always best to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other cyclists, and to carry waste bags.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the By the Tera River — Benavente and the Valles route. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the By the Tera River — Benavente and the Valles route?
faqs.answer: Benavente, a town rich in history and amenities, serves as an excellent access point. You can find parking options within Benavente, which also offers all necessary services for touring cyclists.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat, drink, or find accommodation near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the town of Benavente is well-equipped with services for visitors. You'll find supermarkets, various accommodation options, and restaurants, making it a convenient stopover or base for your cycling adventure.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a touring cycling trip on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's 'difficult' rating and its length (over 30 km), it's advisable to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your touring bicycle, appropriate cycling gear, sun protection, and a first-aid kit. A navigation device with the route loaded is also recommended.
